info.name: Druthers API
info.description:
API for druthers.io — track and rank the movies, TV, books, and games you actually care about.

---

**Auth:** most requests use the collection-level bearer token — set the `apiToken` variable to a personal API key (`drk_…`), minted at https://www.druthers.io/settings, or a JWT from `POST /v1/auth/token`. `POST /v1/auth/token` and `POST /v1/users` are marked "No Auth" since you don't have a token yet when calling them.

**Base URL:** the `baseUrl` variable defaults to production (`https://api.druthers.io`) — override it in a Postman environment to point at localhost (`http://localhost:8000`) or QA (`https://api-qa.druthers.io`) instead.

Generated from the checked-in OpenAPI schema by `scripts/generate_postman_collection.py` — see `docs/mcp-usage.md` for the MCP (Claude) integration instead of raw HTTP calls.

request.description:
Mint a new key. The plaintext secret is in this response and nowhere
else — it is never stored or shown again.
List Api Keys
Revocation is deletion — the hash is gone, the key can never auth again.

item.name: Activity
item.description: Cross-domain activity log and "I'm bored" recommendation

request.description: Cross-domain "what have I been up to" feed, newest first.

Randomly pick one item off the user's watchlists/bucket lists, across
every domain. ``exclude`` (comma-separated entity ids) lets the client
re-roll without repeating the item(s) it's already shown.
Create Book
Delete Book
Get All Books
Return one book's full detail, enriching from Open Library on first view.
Return the current user's tracker for one book (404 if not tracked).

query.description: Only ranked entries (true) or only unranked (false)
Only queued entries (true) or only unqueued (false)

request.description: Get User Books
Add a book to the user's lists (idempotent — merges list membership).
Persist a new ranking order (drag-and-drop). Rank = position in the list.
Search Books Endpoint

Place a book at an exact 1-based position in the ranked list, shifting the
books at and below that position down by one. Works for a not-yet-ranked
book (jump it in) or an already-ranked one (move it).
Unmark Book
Update Book
Update list membership, rank, or notes for a tracked book.
Create Country
Delete Country
Get All Countries
Return one country's detail, enriching from REST Countries on first view.
Get User Countries
Return the current user's tracker for one country (404 if not tracked).
Add a country to the user's lists (idempotent — merges list membership).
Persist a new ranking order (drag-and-drop). Rank = position in the list.

Place a country at an exact 1-based position in the visited ranking,
shifting the countries at and below that position down by one.
Seed/refresh the full world catalog from REST Countries.
Unmark Country
Update Country
Update list membership, rank, notes, or first-visited for a country.

Spreadsheet-friendly CSV for one domain:
``movies`` | ``tv-shows`` | ``tv-episodes`` | ``books`` | ``games``.

Full-fidelity JSON export of every domain, using the same schemas the
API serves — anything the UI can show is in here.

Upload the standard Goodreads library export CSV. Idempotent — re-running
the same file updates rather than duplicates. Returns counts plus any
skipped rows with reasons (nothing is dropped silently).

item.name: Summary
item.description: Home summary — per-shelf Top 5 and counts

request.description:
Per-shelf Top 5 plus ranked/queued counts for the signed-in user.

Replaces the home page's four full-collection fetches; row count is
independent of library size.

item.name: TV
item.description: TV shows, episodes, and per-user tracker

request.description: Create Episode
Create Tv Show
Delete Episode
Delete Tv Show
Get All Episodes
Get All Tv Shows

What to watch: unwatched episodes airing within +/- ``window_days`` of
today, everything overdue and unwatched (catch-up), and shows the user
has frozen (paused tracking on, so they're excluded from both).
Return one show's full detail, enriching from TVMaze on first view.
The current user's episode watch marks for one show.
Return the current user's tracker for one show (404 if not tracked).

query.description: Only ranked entries (true) or only unranked (false)
Only queued entries (true) or only unqueued (false)

request.description: Get User Tv Shows

Mark every episode of a show watched (idempotent), or with ``season``
just that one season's episodes.
Mark an episode watched (idempotent).
Add a show to the user's lists (idempotent — merges list membership).
Persist a new ranking order (drag-and-drop). Rank = position in the list.
Search Tv Shows Endpoint

Place a show at an exact 1-based position in the ranked list, shifting the
shows at and below that position down by one. Works for a not-yet-ranked
show (jump it in) or an already-ranked one (move it).
Refresh the episode list from TVMaze (for ongoing shows).
Unmark Episode Watched
Unmark Tv Show
Update Episode
Update Tv Show
Update list membership, rank, or notes for a tracked show.

request.description: Get User Games
Add a game to the user's lists (idempotent — merges list membership).
Persist a new ranking order (drag-and-drop). Rank = position in the list.
Search Games Endpoint

Place a game at an exact 1-based position in the ranked list, shifting the
games at and below that position down by one. Works for a not-yet-ranked
game (jump it in) or an already-ranked one (move it).
Unmark Game
Update Game
Update list membership, rank, notes, or 100% flag for a tracked game.
Get Visibility

Read-only public profile: ranked lists of the categories the owner has
opted in, best first. Fully private profiles (and unknown handles) 404
identically, so the endpoint never confirms a private account exists.

Update the handle and/or per-category public flags. Opening any category
to the public requires a handle (that's the profile URL).

Retrieves a JWT token if username (email) and password match

Args:
    username: The email of the user
    password: The password of the user

Returns:
    Access token

Sign in with a Google Identity Services ID token.

Verifies the token against the configured Google client id, then upserts the
user (creating one on first sign-in) and returns a JWT — the same shape as
the password flow.
Index endpoint that returns a welcome message.

Create a new user in the database.

Args:
    request (InUserBase): Must include email,
    display_name (max length 16 characters), and password.
    db (Session): The database session.

Returns:
    List: OutUserDisplay: The newly created user data.

Delete a user by ID from the database.
Admins can delete any user, while users can only delete their own account.

Args:
    uuid (str): The ID of the user.
    db (Session): The database session.
    current_user (dict): The current authenticated user.

Returns:
    List: OutUserDisplay: The deleted user data.

Retrieve all users from the database. Must be an admin to view all users.

Args:
    db (Session): The database session.

Returns:
    List: OutUserDisplay: A list of users.

Retrieve a user by ID from the database.
Admins can view all users, while users can only view their own account.

Args:
    uuid (str): The ID of the user.
    db (Session): The database session.

Returns:
    List: OutUserDisplay: The user data.

Update a user's information in the database.
Admins can update any user, while users can only update their own account.

Args:
    uuid (str): The ID of the user.
    request (InUserBase): The updated user data.
    db (Session): The database session.

Returns:
    List: OutUserDisplay: The updated user data.
